Title: Wheat waxy proteins: polymorphism, molecular characterization and effects on starch properties

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Multiple cultivars including Chinese Spring, Kanto107, Bai Huo
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes amylose from ADP-glucose within starch granules in endosperm amyloplasts
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines starch properties affecting glycaemic index, shelf life, and processing quality of wheat products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Grain starch granules in endosperm amyloplasts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Null or reduced-function alleles of Wx-A1 reduce amylose content; complete loss contributes to waxy phenotype when combined with null alleles at Wx-B1 and Wx-D1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Wx-A1 encodes one of the three wheat waxy proteins that are sole enzymes responsible for amylose synthesis in wheat endosperm. 2. Multiple alleles including null, reduced-expression, and amino-acid-substitution variants were catalogued. 3. Loss or reduced activity of Wx-A1 lowers amylose content, but its effect is weaker than Wx-B1 in most studies.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for amylose synthesis; sole enzyme for amylose synthesis in wheat endosperm together with homoeologs
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein electrophoresis; allele sequencing; null mutant analysis; crossing to generate partial and full waxy lines; amylose quantification
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful for breeding partial-waxy and waxy wheat with modified starch properties and reduced amylose; can be stacked with Wx-B1 and Wx-D1 null or reduced-function alleles
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Complete waxy combinations can impair noodle, bread, and pasta quality by making products sticky, soft, or structurally unstable
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Some alleles show reduced expression due to transposable-like element insertions affecting splicing or UTR-mediated expression rather than complete loss of coding function

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Multiple cultivars including Chinese Spring, Kanto107, Bai Huo
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes amylose from ADP-glucose within starch granules in endosperm amyloplasts
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines starch properties affecting glycaemic index, shelf life, and processing quality of wheat products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Grain starch granules in endosperm amyloplasts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Wx-B1 has the strongest individual effect among the three waxy loci on amylose synthesis; null or reduced-function alleles markedly lower amylose content
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Wx-B1 encodes the most influential waxy protein for amylose synthesis among the three homoeologs in many studies. 2. Null and variant alleles were identified across bread wheat, durum wheat, emmer, and spelt. 3. Loss of Wx-B1 commonly causes the largest decrease in amylose among single-locus waxy mutations.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Major key control step among waxy homoeologs; strongest dosage effect on amylose content
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein electrophoresis; allele sequencing; null mutant analysis; breeding of partial waxy lines; amylose quantification
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value target for reducing amylose in breeding; single null allele has strong effect and can be combined with other waxy alleles for tailored starch quality
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Large deletions such as Wx-B1b may remove adjacent quality-related genes; strong amylose reduction can negatively affect some end-use qualities
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The full-gene deletion allele Wx-B1b may encompass nearby quality genes; newer null alleles such as Wx-B1l/k/m may avoid this issue

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Multiple cultivars including Chinese Spring, Bai Huo, Scoutland
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Synthesizes amylose from ADP-glucose within starch granules in endosperm amyloplasts
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Determines starch properties affecting glycaemic index, shelf life, and processing quality of wheat products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Grain starch granules in endosperm amyloplasts
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Null or altered Wx-D1 alleles reduce amylose content and contribute to waxy or partial-waxy starch phenotypes, though usually with smaller effect than Wx-B1
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Wx-D1 is one of the three wheat GBSSI homoeologs required for normal amylose accumulation. 2. Variation at Wx-D1 is lower than at Wx-A1 and Wx-B1, but several null and amino-acid-substitution alleles are known. 3. Null Wx-D1 contributes to partial-waxy and, in combination with Wx-A1b and Wx-B1b, full waxy wheat.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key control step for amylose synthesis; effect generally less than Wx-B1
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Protein electrophoresis; molecular marker analysis; allele sequencing; mutant analysis; amylose quantification
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful in combination with Wx-A1 and Wx-B1 alleles to produce partial- or full-waxy wheat and alter starch functionality
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Very low amylose in stacked null backgrounds can reduce noodle structural integrity and impair bread or pasta quality
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Variation at this locus is relatively limited compared with Wx-A1 and Wx-B1
